quoted_tweet.text:
i think the biggest gap for a long time has been an ideological focus on decentralization 

the most important property to come out of blockchain research has been verifiability not decentralization

hyperliquid showed that a (semi)centralized system offers compelling advantages over fully decentralized system

but hyperliquid built fully-trusted black-box with a fugazi validator set to signal its decentralized, rather than build a verifiable system  

a fully-verifiable, trustless nasdaq is more compelling than a fully decentralized black-box nasdaq. and its not just compelling to crypto-twitter. regulations would be easier to enforce with a verifiable systems we can audit in real-time. verifiability allows institutions with compliance requirements to prove every piece of the system operates as describes  

a wild-west financial world sounds without clear rules sounds like a dystopian world retail users can't navigate. a fully-verifiable financial world, where everyone can verify everyone else is playing by the same rules is the world we should strive towards 

the interesting problems today are how to build verifiable systems to replace early black-box trading and banking systems that were built pre-internet

verifiability is what is going will allow crypto and world to scale faster

and this doesn't just apply to finance. as we rely on AI to do critical tasks on our behalf, verifying the input/outputs to AI models is a problem that needs to be solved
